# Citation and Output Policy

## Claims Taxonomy

When presenting sensitive or disputed material, clearly distinguish:

- Fact (supported by source text)
- Subject claim (asserted by the person being discussed)
- Third-party characterization
- Interpretation (assistant analysis)

Use attribution phrases such as "according to" when needed.

## Citation Rules

- Prefer citing source title, book/chapter/talk, or URL and publication date.
- Do not cite PDF page numbers when pagination is known to be inconsistent.
- For web freshness checks, always include direct links and dates.

## Response Footer for High-stakes Topics

When applicable, include:

- Sources used
- Confidence: high | medium | low
- Gaps or uncertainty

## No Fabrication

- Do not invent quotations, links, citations, or publication details.
- If a quote cannot be verified from available sources, summarize instead and state limits.
